title: Apple Health ingestion (SAX streaming) + `bober medical import`
description: Implement bounded, streaming ingestion. IngestionNormalizer holds an IngestionAdapter registry and drives importFile through the matching adapter into the HealthDataStore via an ObservationSink. AppleHealthAdapter stream-parses Apple Health export XML via SAX with bounded (~1000-row) batches and pause/resume backpressure, never loading the whole ~4GB document into memory. A `bober medical import <file>` CLI command wires it end-to-end. importFile throws a clear error when no adapter canHandle the file.

successCriteria.description: The project builds with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build).
TypeScript strict-mode typecheck passes with zero errors (npm run typecheck).
All tests pass including new ingestion tests against a fixture Apple Health XML; the full pre-existing suite stays green.

AppleHealthAdapter ingests a fixture Apple Health export XML via STREAMING SAX (no whole-file read): a test asserts the parser is fed via a read stream and that batches are flushed at a bounded cap (e.g. <=1000 rows per writeBatch).

Backpressure: the ObservationSink.writeBatch is awaited and the parse stream pauses/resumes around it so unbounded rows do not buffer in memory — asserted by a test using a slow sink that the parser waits on.
Re-importing the same file is idempotent: the second import adds 0 new rows (dedup via HealthDataStore INSERT OR IGNORE), asserted against the store row count.
IngestionNormalizer.importFile throws a clear error naming the problem when no registered adapter canHandle the file; register() adds adapters that canHandle dispatches to.
`bober medical import <file>` is registered in the CLI, callable, and reports an IngestionResult (e.g. new-row / total counts) on completion; a test or smoke invocation exercises the command wiring.

nonGoals: Do not add Whoop or CSV adapters — Apple Health XML only (the registry makes them additive later).
Do not implement EgressGuard, medications, or the full SOP wiring (Sprint 6).
Do not parse medications from the file into HealthDataStore (medications are FactStore value-of-record — Sprint 6 / ADR-7).
Do not load the whole document into memory at any point.
stopConditions: Apple Health XML streams via SAX with bounded batches + backpressure, re-import is idempotent, unknown files throw, and `bober medical import` works end-to-end
definitionOfDone: src/medical/ gains IngestionNormalizer (register/importFile + adapter registry), an ObservationSink that batches into HealthDataStore.upsertObservations, and AppleHealthAdapter (SAX streaming, bounded batches, backpressure). A `bober medical import <file>` CLI command (registered like src/cli/commands/run.ts) drives importFile and prints the IngestionResult. Build, typecheck, and all tests pass.
assumptions: A streaming SAX XML parser is available (e.g. an existing dep; if none, add a small well-known SAX dep such as 'sax' — justify in the PR and keep it light). Apple Health exports use <Record type="..." unit="..." value="..." startDate="..." endDate="..." sourceName="..."/> elements.
Record.type maps to HealthObservation.metric, value->value (parseFloat), unit->unit, startDate->tStart, endDate->tEnd, sourceName->source 'apple-health'. Non-numeric records are skipped.
writeBatch is async and awaited; the SAX stream is paused while a batch is flushing and resumed after (backpressure). Batch cap ~1000.
IngestionResult reports counts (recordsParsed, newRows) and is returned by importFile.
generatorNotes:
Add src/medical/ingestion.ts (IngestionNormalizer + ObservationSink + IngestionAdapter interface), src/medical/adapters/apple-health.ts (AppleHealthAdapter), and a CLI command src/cli/commands/medical.ts (or extend an existing command registrar) for `bober medical import <file>`. Collocate tests.

IngestionNormalizer: register(adapter) pushes to an array; importFile(filePath) finds the first adapter where canHandle(filePath) is true (e.g. by extension/sniff), else throw new Error(`No ingestion adapter can handle '${filePath}'`). Then await adapter.ingest(filePath, sink) and return its IngestionResult.

ObservationSink.writeBatch(obs, labs): await store.upsertObservations(obs) (sync call wrapped) + lab upsert; accumulate counts. Keep the sink small.

AppleHealthAdapter.ingest: open a read stream (fs.createReadStream) and pipe into a SAX parser (streaming, event-based). On each <Record> open tag, map attributes to a HealthObservation, push into a buffer. When buffer.length >= 1000: parser.pause() (or stream.pause()), await sink.writeBatch(batch, []), clear buffer, parser.resume(). On stream end, flush the remainder. NEVER fs.readFile the whole file. canHandle: filePath endsWith '.xml' and (optionally) a quick header sniff for 'HealthData' / 'export.xml'.

CLI: mirror src/cli/commands/run.ts registration. `bober medical import <file>`: build the medical HealthDataStore path under .bober/medical/health.db, construct the store + normalizer + AppleHealthAdapter, call importFile, print the IngestionResult counts, close the store.

FIXTURE: create a small test fixture Apple Health XML (a handful of <Record> elements) under a test dir; a test asserts streaming (spy that fs.readFile is NOT called for the whole file; or assert createReadStream is used), bounded batches (cap), backpressure (a sink that delays and a test that the parser awaited it), idempotent re-import (run twice, second newRows===0), unknown-file throw, and the CLI command exists + runs on the fixture.

If adding the 'sax' dep, keep it isolated to the adapter file and ensure it has no network surface (it does not).
evaluatorNotes:
sc-5-4/5-5: run AppleHealthAdapter.ingest on the fixture with a sink that records batch sizes and introduces an await delay; assert every batch size <= the cap and that ingest awaited the slow sink (e.g. ordering/timing or a pause/resume spy). Assert the adapter uses createReadStream, not fs.readFile, on the whole file.
sc-5-6: import the fixture twice into the same store; assert second IngestionResult.newRows === 0 and the store row count is unchanged.
sc-5-7: importFile on a '.bin' file with no matching adapter throws an Error whose message contains the path.
sc-5-8: invoke the `bober medical import <fixture>` command wiring (programmatically or via the CLI test harness) and assert it returns/prints counts and exits cleanly.
Full suite + build + typecheck green; confirm S4 store dedup is what provides idempotency.

iterationHistory.note: first generator attempt crashed on transient API socket error; recovered via focused lint-fix+commit
